svg.title: BI Assessment Agent — technical stack diagram
svg.desc: A diagram showing all technical tools used in each layer of the BI Assessment Agent, from Docker container through Python agents to output

g.text: Runs on any machine — one command to start

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Why do we use Docker for this project?')

g.text: Orchestrates the full pipeline — entry point is main.py

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does config.py do in the project?')
sendPrompt('What goes in the .env file?')
sendPrompt('What does main.py do?')
sendPrompt('What is in requirements.txt?')

g.text: Files mounted from your machine via docker-compose volumes

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is a TMDL file and how do I export one from Power BI?')
sendPrompt('What CSV files should I put in the inputs folder?')
sendPrompt('What are the three knowledge base MD files?')

g.text: Reads all input files and converts them to structured text

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does the extractor parse a TMDL file?')
sendPrompt('How does the extractor parse CSV files?')
sendPrompt('What format does the extractor output?')

g.text: Retrieves relevant knowledge, then compares model against it

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does LlamaIndex work for RAG in this project?')
sendPrompt('How does AutoGen orchestrate the assessment agent?')
sendPrompt('Which LLM does the assessor use and why?')

g.text: Turns findings into scores, gaps, and opportunities

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does the scorer assign dimension scores?')
sendPrompt('How does severity weighting work in the scorer?')
sendPrompt('How does the scorer decide what is buildable?')

g.text: Written to output/reports/ — mounted back to your machine

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does the final assessment report look like?')
sendPrompt('Why do we output Markdown and not HTML or PDF in v1?')
sendPrompt('How is the output file named?')
